project.name: Event Trigger Webhook Inbox — Build Spec
project.description: Build the hosted webhook inbox that normalizes Linear/GitHub events into internal wake envelopes for the Workflow Runner to drain.
project.content:
# Event Trigger Webhook Inbox — Build Spec

## Objective
Build the hosted webhook inbox that turns external events (Linear project state changes and GitHub webhooks) into normalized internal "wake" envelopes the Workflow Runner can drain, so decomposition becomes the first automated trigger without being a one-off special case.

## Product Principle
Triggers are wake-up signals, not decision-makers. The inbox authenticates, dedupes, normalizes, enqueues, and exposes status — it never reads or mutates source-of-truth state and never decides which workflow runs. Every meaningful event yields a visible disposition (accepted / deduped / refused / enqueued / ignored).

## Scope
- A hosted HTTP endpoint that receives Linear and GitHub webhooks.
- Per-provider signature verification.
- Idempotent dedupe by provider delivery id.
- Normalization into the internal wake envelope.
- A durable queue the Workflow Runner drains.
- An operator-visible status surface.

## Out of scope
- Evaluating product intent, summarizing/rewriting prose, or choosing a decomposition.
- Any mutation of Linear or GitHub.
- The Workflow Runner's gating/decision logic (the runner owns that).

## Architecture / Technical context (authoritative — decompose against this)
- Runtime & repo: a Node 20 service under execution/integrations/inbox/ (new), deployed as a Supabase Edge Function alongside the existing teami-inbox function; reuse the repo's existing config + credential-store conventions.
- Endpoint: POST /v1/webhooks/:provider (provider in {linear, github}). 202 on accept/enqueue, 200 on dedupe/ignored, 401 on bad signature, 422 on unparseable payload.
- Verification: Linear verifies the Linear-Signature header against the stored webhook secret; GitHub verifies X-Hub-Signature-256 (HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body). Secrets come from the existing credential store and are never logged.
- Dedupe: key on (provider, delivery_id) — Linear delivery / GitHub X-GitHub-Delivery — via an inbox_deliveries Postgres table with a unique constraint; a duplicate insert yields 200 deduped with no enqueue.
- Normalization: map the provider payload to the wake envelope { id, object_id, workflow_type, trigger_type, source_provider, source_object_id, received_at }. A Linear Project -> Planned transition maps to workflow_type "decomposition", trigger_type "linear.project.planned". Unknown event types are recorded and ignored (200), not enqueued.
- Queue: persist the wake envelope to an inbox_wake_queue Postgres table (status in {queued, leased, done, dead}); the Workflow Runner leases with the existing 300s lease + 120s heartbeat conventions. No external broker.
- Status surface: GET /v1/status returns recent deliveries with their disposition and the current queue depth.
- Contracts to honor: the wake envelope shape above is the Workflow Runner's input contract (do not change it); the inbox is read-only against Linear/GitHub; all writes are to the two inbox tables only.

## Acceptance evidence
- Every inbound request produces exactly one recorded delivery with a disposition.
- A replayed delivery id never produces a second queue entry.
- A Linear Project -> Planned event produces exactly one decomposition wake envelope in the queue.
- A tampered or invalid signature is refused with 401 and recorded, never enqueued.
- An operator can see, per delivery, what happened and why.
